title: Include generated assets in every Go release target
prompt: The release pipeline in scripts/build-release.sh successfully cross-compiles distill for linux/amd64 and windows/amd64, but the Windows executable does not contain the generated index asset. Fix the target-specific packaging defect so every configured release artifact uses the same embedded generated assets through internal/assets.Lookup. Keep web/ as the source of truth, keep go generate deterministic, keep the checked-in generated output, keep release artifacts reproducible, and preserve the release script's clean-tree guard so it rejects stale output before writing artifacts. Do not replace embedded lookup with a runtime filesystem fallback. Do not modify the tests. Run `env HOME=/tmp PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 tests/verify.py`; verification must remain offline and deterministic.
constraints: All commands and tests must run offline.
Both linux/amd64 and windows/amd64 release artifacts must contain the generated index asset.
Asset generation must remain byte-for-byte reproducible.
Release artifact builds must remain byte-for-byte reproducible.
The release build must continue rejecting stale checked-in generated assets before it writes artifacts.
Runtime asset reads must continue to use internal/assets.Lookup and the embedded bundle.
